task_completion.description: Locate the 'front_ir' DistanceSensor on the MICROMOUSE robot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Webots GUI or a text editor is open, showing the scene tree or world file with the MICROMOUSE robot's devices expanded, and the 'front_ir' DistanceSensor is visible or selected.
The 'type' field of the 'front_ir' DistanceSensor is explicitly set to "infra-red" in the Webots field editor or text editor.

task_completion.description: Access the lookupTable field for the 'front_ir' sensor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'lookupTable' field under the 'front_ir' sensor is expanded in the Webots GUI, or the cursor is positioned at the lookupTable array in a text editor.

task_completion.description: Enter the four specific calibration points into the lookup table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The lookup table contains exactly these four coordinate triplets: [0.04, 1023, 0.0], [0.10, 800, 0.02], [0.20, 400, 0.05], and [0.30, 250, 0.08].

task_completion.description: Save the modified world file to the specified Desktop path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save World As...' dialog showing the path '/home/ga/Desktop/micromouse_calibrated.wbt', a terminal command saving the file to that path, or the application window title showing 'micromouse_calibrated.wbt'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with Webots or a text editor to modify the world file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Webots interface or a text editor being actively used to edit the .wbt file, rather than just running a pre-made script.

integrity.description: The agent manually entered or configured the lookup table points.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing or adding the specific values into the lookup table fields, rather than downloading a completed file.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely saved the file to the correct location.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action is visible via GUI dialogs or editor commands, not just a terminal 'touch' command to create an empty file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target sensor is 'front_ir'. The sensor type must be exactly 'infra-red'. The lookup table must contain exactly these four points (distance, ADC, noise): [0.04, 1023.0, 0.0], [0.1, 800.0, 0.02], [0.2, 400.0, 0.05], [0.3, 250.0, 0.08]. The final file must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/micromouse_calibrated.wbt.
